heading: Pyth-1 Koenig layer isolated and Pyth-2 pipeline started
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth1, pyth2, koenig, table4, corpus-ingestion]
body: Built `pyth1_koenig_validated_layer.json` as a dedicated Pyth-1 Table 4 / Koenig artifact using only stable validated sources. It contains 5 validated transitions: block transitions `1->7`, `7->17`, `17->193` from `pyth1_p080_l6106`, and the branching successors `193->497` and `193->599` from `pyth1_p080_l6108`. The OCR-damaged backward trace item remains quarantined in the Pyth-1 holdout lane. Started the same controlled pipeline for Pyth-2: `build_pyth2_workbook.py` produced queues/workbook with counts `3 accepted / 2 OCR cleanup / 7 manual review`; `build_pyth2_reinterpretations.py` produced `pyth2_reinterpretation_batch_001.json` for the clean page-110 items; `build_pyth2_registers.py` and `build_pyth2_validated_workset.py` promoted that into a first stable lane of 3 items (2 direct validated, 1 corrected-source validated) with 1 flagged corrected-source holdout. The validated Pyth-2 items cover fixed `e=60` admissible b-counting, symmetry of admissible pairs about 30, and the every-fifth Fibonacci divisibility rule for 5.
